title: fix port list by container with port
body:
code was erronously misinterpretting the port as a containername.

Fixes: #1791832

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [baude] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Would you mind adding a quick test to `test/system/500-networking.bats`, just before cleanup?
Can you add a test to make sure we don't have a regression in the future.
Friendly PR suggestion (tests, and a help-message fix): https://github.com/baude/libpod/pull/30
I've tried repeatedly to restart tests on this; they always fail. @baude, maybe a rebase will get rid of the test failures? Also, I do hope you'll consider my friendly-amendment PR.
udpated review comments and grabbed @edsantiago PR commit with tests
Changes LGTM, but tests are red
@baude ping, this should be a simple one-character fix

One looks like a flake, but it's podman-remote and not worth pursuing:
```
Error: could not get runtime: dial unix /run/user/14339/io.podman-b7130f4d25d2c6c0e7e65ce34f5b6ecd4340af64fae3111c90f0817f36a8fa77: connect: no such file or directory
```
The other, sigh, is more podman-remote b0rkage. Simple fix: in `500-networking.bats`, add:
```diff
 @test "podman networking: port on localhost" {
+    skip_if_remote
     random_1=...
```
I'm sorry this one has been so painful. I should've been the one pushing and testing that networking test.
@edsantiago no problem at all! i'm just happy to have the help.
